summary: Validated new distributor-delivery status lifecycle end-to-end. Backend: 7/7 pytest cases pass exercising Draft -> Confirmed -> delivery_assigned (attach) -> delivery_scheduled (approve) -> on_the_way (driver start) -> complete (driver complete-stop), the direct Stock-Out 'Confirmed -> complete' path, detach-revert-to-confirmed, and summary endpoint counting 'complete' + legacy 'delivered'. Frontend: implementation review confirms (1) LiveDriverMap wrapped in collapsible Card with mapExpanded=useState(false) — collapsed by default — and the toggle button carries data-testid='live-map-toggle' on a card with data-testid='live-map-card' (only rendered when schedule.driver_id is set AND status in approved|in_progress|completed); (2) DistributorDetail.getDeliveryStatusBadge maps delivery_assigned->indigo 'Delivery Assigned', delivery_scheduled->violet 'Delivery Scheduled', on_the_way->amber 'On the Way', complete->green 'Complete', and keeps legacy 'delivered' for older rows. UI screenshot of /distributor-home (logged in as john.distributor@test.com) shows the new 'COMPLETE' green badges on Recent Deliveries for the rows just transitioned to 'complete' by the backend lifecycle tests, alongside legacy 'DELIVERED' badges on older rows — confirming the badge renderer reads the new status correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: distributor_delivery_schedules.attach_deliveries (line ~756-770) is fine but the 'lift delivery_assigned -> delivery_scheduled when schedule already approved' path is double-work after the preceding update_many; could be a single update with $in. Minor perf only.

driver_app.complete_stop returns {already_delivered: true} for legacy 'delivered' rows which silently no-ops auto-completion — acceptable but means the schedule may not auto-flip to 'completed' if mixed legacy stops are present.

distributors.complete_delivery accepts 'in_transit' as a startable state (line 4457) but the new flow never produces 'in_transit' anymore; harmless but could be tightened to ['confirmed','on_the_way','delivery_assigned','delivery_scheduled'].

success_rate.frontend: Static code + visual badge spot-check passed; live-map toggle interaction not exercised end-to-end (no approved schedule with driver_id+map data persisted at test time).

test_credentials: Admin surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth/test123 and Distributor john.distributor@test.com/nyladist## per /app/memory/test_credentials.md. A throwaway TEST_FLOW driver is provisioned per test run and cleaned up.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates: 1 stock-out delivery (-> complete), 1 lifecycle delivery (-> complete via driver flow), 1 schedule (cancelled+deleted in teardown), 1 admin driver (deleted in teardown). All test deliveries are tagged with reference_number starting 'TEST_FLOW-'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_delivery_status_flow.py. Run with: python -m pytest backend/tests/test_delivery_status_flow.py -v. It self-bootstraps a TEST_FLOW driver via /api/admin/drivers, logs in via /api/driver/login, and exercises the entire status chain. Frontend collapse toggle was inspected via source (mapExpanded=false default, data-testid='live-map-toggle'/'live-map-card' present) but a click-interaction E2E was not run because no approved+driver-assigned schedule with live pings was persisted (lifecycle test cleans up its own schedule). To deepen coverage: keep one lifecycle schedule alive (don't cancel) and Playwright-click the toggle to verify Google Maps iframe mounts after expand.
